items.name: github
items.description: The official GitHub MCP server: creating issues, managing pull requests, code review, searching across repositories.
items.useFor: Developers. For example: "Find all open PRs with failing tests and leave comments with fixes."

items.name: linear
items.description: Integration with the Linear tracker: creating tasks, managing projects, updating statuses, searching across workspaces.
items.useFor: Product teams. For example: "Create tasks in Linear from this list of bugs and spread them across the sprint."

items.name: notion
items.description: Integration with Notion: searching pages, creating and updating documents, working with databases and the team knowledge base.
items.useFor: Teams with a knowledge base in Notion. For example: "Find our onboarding guide and update the section on access."

items.name: asana
items.description: Integration with Asana: creating and managing tasks, searching across projects, assigning owners, tracking progress.
items.useFor: Project managers. For example: "Show my overdue tasks and create a status update for the project."

items.name: atlassian
items.description: Connecting to Jira and Confluence: searching and creating tasks, accessing documentation, managing sprints.
items.useFor: Teams on the Atlassian stack. For example: "Turn these requirements into an epic with tasks in Jira and a page in Confluence."

items.name: slack
items.description: Integration with Slack: searching messages, accessing channels, reading threads and team conversations.
items.useFor: Anyone who works in Slack. For example: "Find the discussion of yesterday's outage in #incidents and give me a short summary."

items.name: zapier
items.description: Connects 8,000+ apps through Zapier: search, enable, and run actions right from Claude.
items.useFor: No-code automation. For example: "When an email with an invoice attachment arrives, save it to Google Drive and log it in a spreadsheet."

items.name: figma
items.description: Integration with Figma: accessing design files, extracting component information, reading design tokens, turning mockups into code.
items.useFor: Frontend developers and designers. For example: "Take this mockup from Figma and build a pixel-perfect React component."

items.name: canva
items.description: Creating, editing, resizing, and brand-checking Canva designs through the Canva MCP server.
items.useFor: Marketers and social media managers. For example: "Make a series of social posts from this announcement and bring them in line with our brand guidelines."

items.name: adobe-for-creativity
items.description: Adobe's creative AI tools: image editing, design-process automation, working with vector graphics and video.
items.useFor: Designers. For example: "Remove the background from these 20 product photos and bring them to a consistent style."

items.name: aws-core
items.description: Building, deploying, and operating applications on AWS: infrastructure as code, core services, common tasks.
items.useFor: DevOps and backend developers. For example: "Write a CloudFormation template for an API on Lambda + DynamoDB and deploy it."

items.name: azure
items.description: Turns Claude into an Azure expert: the Azure MCP server plus specialized skills for working with Microsoft's cloud.
items.useFor: Teams on Azure. For example: "Deploy this app to Azure App Service and set up CI/CD."

items.name: cloudflare
items.description: Cloudflare platform skills: Workers, Durable Objects, the Agents SDK, MCP servers, the Wrangler CLI, and web performance.
items.useFor: Edge developers. For example: "Write a Cloudflare Worker that caches API responses and deploy it with Wrangler."

items.name: vercel
items.description: Integration with the Vercel platform: managing deployments, build statuses, logs, and domain configuration.
items.useFor: Frontend teams. For example: "Why did the last deployment fail? Look at the build logs and fix the error."

items.name: terraform
items.description: The Terraform MCP server: integration with the Terraform ecosystem for automating infrastructure as code.
items.useFor: DevOps engineers. For example: "Describe our infrastructure in Terraform and find the current provider versions in the registry."

items.name: mongodb
items.description: The official MongoDB plugin (MCP server + skills): connecting to databases, exploring data, managing collections, optimizing queries.
items.useFor: Backend developers. For example: "Analyze the slow queries against the orders collection and suggest indexes."

items.name: supabase
items.description: Supabase MCP integration: database operations, authentication, file storage, and realtime subscriptions.
items.useFor: Fullstack developers. For example: "Create a profiles table with RLS policies and generate TypeScript types."

items.name: firebase
items.description: Google Firebase MCP integration: Firestore, authentication, cloud functions, hosting, and storage.
items.useFor: Mobile and web developers. For example: "Set up Firestore security rules and write a cloud function for notifications."

items.name: neon
items.description: Managing Neon projects and databases (serverless Postgres) through an agent skill and the Neon MCP server.
items.useFor: Postgres developers. For example: "Create a database branch for tests, run a migration, and merge it back."

items.name: sentry
items.description: Integration with Sentry: accessing error reports, analyzing stack traces, searching issues, debugging production incidents.
items.useFor: Developers on production duty. For example: "Go through the top 5 errors in Sentry over the last day and suggest fixes."

items.name: datadog
items.description: Datadog right inside Claude Code through a preconfigured MCP server: querying logs, metrics, traces, and dashboards.
items.useFor: SRE and DevOps. For example: "Why did API latency go up after the deploy? Compare metrics and traces before and after."

items.name: grafana-mcp
items.description: The Grafana MCP server: managing dashboards, data sources, alerts, and incidents with AI.
items.useFor: Monitoring teams. For example: "Create a dashboard for our service's metrics and set up an alert for a rise in errors."

items.name: posthog
items.description: Access to PostHog analytics: insights, feature flags, experiments, and error tracking right from Claude Code.
items.useFor: Product teams. For example: "What's the signup funnel conversion for the month? Build an insight and turn on a feature flag for a test."

items.name: stripe
items.description: The Stripe plugin for building payment integrations: working with the payments, subscriptions, and customers APIs.
items.useFor: Payments developers. For example: "Wire up Stripe Checkout subscription payments in our app and handle the webhooks."

items.name: playwright
items.description: Microsoft's browser-automation MCP server: Claude can open pages, click, fill in forms, and take screenshots.
items.useFor: QA and developers. For example: "Walk through the checkout flow on staging and show me where it breaks."

items.name: chrome-devtools-mcp
items.description: Driving a live Chrome browser from Claude: performance recordings, network request analysis, page debugging.
items.useFor: Frontend developers. For example: "Open our site, record a performance trace, and find what's slowing down the first paint."

items.name: context7
items.description: Upstash Context7: up-to-date documentation for libraries and frameworks with code examples for a specific version — right in Claude's context.
items.useFor: All developers. For example: "Write a migration to Next.js 15 — pull the current APIs from Context7, not from the model's memory."

items.name: code-review
items.description: Automatic code review of pull requests by several specialized agents, with confidence scoring to filter out false positives.
items.useFor: Development teams. For example: "Review PR #142 and leave comments only on real problems."

items.name: commit-commands
items.description: Commands for git workflows: commit, push, and create pull requests with a single command.
items.useFor: Anyone who works with git. For example: /commit-commands:commit — stages changes, generates a message, and creates a commit.

items.name: security-guidance
items.description: Security checks for the code Claude writes: pattern-based warnings on edits and an LLM review of the diff for vulnerabilities.
items.useFor: Every development team. Works automatically: Claude finds and fixes vulnerabilities in its own changes within the same session.

items.name: mcp-server-dev
items.description: Skills for designing and building MCP servers that work well with Claude: choosing a deployment model, designing tools.
items.useFor: Integration developers. For example: "Design an MCP server for our CRM — help me choose between a local and a remote option."

items.name: typescript-lsp
items.description: The TypeScript/JavaScript language server: Claude sees type errors right after edits, can jump to definitions and find references.
items.useFor: TypeScript developers. Claude notices and fixes type errors in the same turn, without running the compiler. Requires typescript-language-server installed.

items.name: superpowers
items.description: The best-known community plugin: a complete development methodology for the agent — spec, plan, TDD, subagent-driven development.
items.useFor: Developers who want the agent to work like a disciplined engineer rather than jump straight to writing code.

items.name: claude-hud
items.description: A real-time HUD status line for Claude Code: context health, tool activity, agent tracking, and todo progress.
items.useFor: Power users of Claude Code — you can see how much context is left and what the agent is doing, right in the status line.

items.name: claude-usage-statusline
items.description: Shows usage-limit percentages in the status line (5-hour session, week, context window), a countdown, and burn rate.
items.useFor: Subscription users — so you don't unexpectedly hit a limit in the middle of a task.

items.name: testdino
items.description: TestDino in Claude Code through a hosted MCP server: viewing test runs, debugging failing tests, managing manual tests.
items.useFor: QA teams. For example: "Why did the nightly e2e run fail? Go through the report and find the flaky tests."
